transcript.clean_text: Every stage of the Giro d'Italia is live and on demand on GCN Plus. After the first rest day, drama at the Giro d'Italia. More rain falling, but before a pedal was turned, Remco Evenepoel, race leader, out with COVID. Joining him, Domenico Pozzovivo and several others. Geraint Thomas would take the pink jersey. And the race recovering from all of those shocks as the rain fell and they'd start a really hard stage. There were rumors, in fact, that the stage wouldn't be raced at all. Perhaps only the last 70 flat kilometers. That wouldn't be the case in the end. Racing full on from the start as they wove their way over the Tuscan and Emiliano hills to the Tuscan seaside and a finish in Viareggio after almost 200 km. Alessandro De Marchi was one of many riders trying to get up the road. Derek Gee joined him. Behind, more problems and another GC favorite on his way out. Aleksandr Vlasov feeling ill. He was distanced early on and would climb off a couple of hours into the race. Magnus Cort would get up to the front. Edward Theuns, two riders joining the other two, four with a 4-minute gap with 150 Ks to go. Behind, there'd be movement at the intermediate sprint and that movement going in the favor of Jonathan Milan just about getting the better of Michael Matthews. The weather was horrific. Lots of changes of clothes, drier rain jackets needed, new arm warmers, leg warmers, and a chance to change, but some of those changes would pose problems. Fernando Gaviria as Movistar been riding all day changing clothes, but over the top, having to chase back on, and he would then crash. He'd get back on his bike, but not regain contact with the front of the race. At the front of the peloton, Damiano Caruso slipping away alongside Jonathan Milan. The chase behind causing problems for Jay Vine. He would lose contact with the Maglia Rosa group and end up losing 10 minutes, dropping out of the top 10 in the GC. Caruso, Milan and company would be caught by what was left of the Maglia Rosa group, thinning all the time. More crashes on the way as the breakaway stayed away. Silly crashes, too. People running into the road, arguments, tempers flaring. And Alberto Bettiol having every right to be angry. Warren Barguil went down, he was hurting. But he would soldier on. Question was, for how long? The biggest question being posed at the front of the race, would the breakaway be caught? It was swinging the way of the peloton. Mark Cavendish made his way back on. Several other riders were with him, but not all of the sprinters, all their domestiques had made it. 15 km to go and almost a minute still for those in the breakaway. Riders' firepower running up behind. A great opportunity for the likes of Cavendish, Pedersen and company. Jonathan Milan was eyeing up a second win as well, but they would not catch the break. And the breakaway staying away, that back becoming clear with around 3 to 4 km to go. So, inside the last 1,500 m, the first attack from Derek Gee from Canada. Already won second in this Giro d'Italia. Alessandro De Marchi distanced, back, and trying again inside the final 300 m. He'd be caught. Gee closing the gap. Caught keeping his powder dry and the fastest to the finish. A second stage victory from a breakaway for his team EF Education-EasyPost. And the Grand Tour's trilogy complete. stages at the welter tour and now stage of the Giro for Magnus Cort. Behind his compatriot Magnus Cort winning the rest of the bunch sprint. Geraint Thomas making sure he would keep the maglia rosa. A delight for Magnus Cort taking his reward after going for it in the breakaway. Danish delight and Cort becoming the second Dane in this race to complete the grand tour set of victories. Stage 10 ending with Geraint Thomas taking his first proper pink jersey. A narrow lead at the top and the GC fight on but movements down the bottom end of the top 10. Ineos Grenadiers now having four riders in the top 10 after what happened with the likes of Alexandr Vlasov and Jay Vine dropping down. Eddie Dunbar inside the top nine as well. A big transition stage and next up stage 11. The longest stage of this 106th Giro d'Italia. 219 km crossing three regions and leaving Tuscany heading into Liguria before finishing in Piemonte. And finishing in Fossano and Sestri Ponente in Tortona. The first stage visit since a victory for Fernando Gaviria a few years back. I remind you you can watch the Giro d'Italia on GCN Plus each and every day live and on demand.
